conversation.user: Taking orgo for the first time. Exam in 2 weeks. McMurry Ch.10-15 (180 pages). I've been going to lectures but not really studying outside of class. I have about 1.5 hours a day.

mustAsk: Are there practice problems or past exams from the professor?

strategy: Track B (Problems): immediate chapter-end problems (2-3) after each reading session — from Day 1
Day 13-14: mechanism review + timed mock problems using remaining 180min buffer
interleaved reading + mechanism drawing (stop and draw on paper when mechanisms appear)
warnings: 7.0 min/page calculation exhausts entire budget with 0 time for problems — do NOT use
reading only without problem practice = likely exam failure despite full coverage
Ch.15 may not be fully covered if reading goes slow — Ch.15 mechanics-only skim as fallback

mustIdentify: Track B (Problems): immediate chapter-end problems (2-3) after each reading session — from Day 1

emotion.toneExpected: matter-of-fact; normalize 6min/page as expected for first-time orgo; practical dual-track plan

hardFailChecks: must NOT lower min/page below 6.0 to make budget appear comfortable — v3 error was using 7.0 (budget exhaustion)
must include problem-solving Track B — reading alone is insufficient
must NOT give same plan as TC-34 (retake) or TC-35 (senior)
